title: Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese

seo.title: How to Make Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese — Easy Chinese Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese. This easy Chinese recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese recipe

seo.h1: Authentic Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese Recipe

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Chinese Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese — a beloved Chinese classic

description: Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese is a classic dish revered for its delicate flavors and tender texture. This traditional recipe highlights the freshness of the fish, complemented by aromatic ginger, scallions, and a savory soy sauce dressing. It's a simple yet elegant preparation that embodies the essence of Cantonese home cooking.

ingredients.item: Whole white fish (e.g., snapper, branzino, striped bass)

ingredients.notes: half cut into 2-inch sections, half julienned

ingredients.item: Cooking oil (e.g., peanut or vegetable oil)

instructions.title: Prepare the Fish
instructions.text: Rinse the cleaned fish thoroughly under cold water. Pat it completely dry with paper towels. Make two or three diagonal slashes on both sides of the fish, about 1/2 inch deep. This helps the fish cook evenly and absorb flavors. Place the fish on a heatproof plate that fits inside your steamer.

instructions.title: Arrange Steaming Aromatics
instructions.text: Place the ginger slices and the 2-inch sections of green onions into the fish cavity and scatter some over and under the fish on the plate. These aromatics will infuse flavor into the fish as it steams and help to remove any fishy odor.

instructions.title: Steam the Fish
instructions.text: Bring a large pot or wok with about 2-3 inches of water to a rolling boil. Carefully place the plate with the fish onto a steaming rack inside the pot, ensuring the water does not touch the plate. Cover tightly and steam for 8-12 minutes, depending on the fish's thickness. The fish is done when the flesh turns opaque and flakes easily with a fork.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dressing
instructions.text: While the fish is steaming, combine the light soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, and a pinch of white pepper in a small bowl. Stir until the sugar is dissolved. Set aside.

instructions.title: Finish the Fish
instructions.text: Once steamed, carefully remove the fish plate from the steamer. Discard the cooked ginger slices and green onion sections from the fish and the plate. They have served their purpose. Carefully drain any accumulated liquid from the plate; this liquid can be fishy.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Arrange the fresh julienned ginger and green onions evenly over the top of the steamed fish. Sprinkle with fresh cilantro. Heat the cooking oil in a small pan until it's very hot and just begins to smoke. Carefully pour the hot oil over the julienned aromatics on the fish; you should hear a sizzling sound. Immediately pour the prepared soy sauce dressing around the fish (not directly on top of the crispy aromatics). Serve immediately with steamed white rice.

tips: Always use the freshest possible whole fish for steaming. Its delicate flavor is the star of this dish.
Do not overcook the fish; it should be just cooked through and still moist. A thicker fish might need an extra minute or two, while a thinner one will cook faster.
The hot oil sizzles the fresh aromatics, releasing their fragrance and adding a crucial layer of flavor and aroma to the final dish.
article: Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese is a beloved Chinese Seafood dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
